qa_pairs.question: What is the Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Canadian Common CV (CCV) is a software system funded by the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) that standardizes CV submissions to granting agencies for funding competitions.

qa_pairs.question: Which major funding agencies currently use the CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Major funding agencies currently using the CCV include NSERC, SSHRC, NCE, CIHR itself, and the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the Alliance require faculty users to submit a CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance requires a CCV for all faculty users to renew their Alliance account and to apply for Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: For whom is submitting a CCV optional when renewing an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: Submitting a CCV is optional for Emeritus Professors, Librarians, and Retired Faculty when renewing their Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Is CCV submission mandatory for all Principal Investigators (PIs) and co-PIs of a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, submission of the CCV is mandatory for all PIs and co-PIs of a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for technical support regarding CCV username or password issues?
qa_pairs.answer: For CCV username or password issues, or technical access support for ccv-cvc.ca, contact the CCV helpdesk directly at support@cihr-irsc.gc.ca or by phone at 613-954-1968 or 1-888-603-4178.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted for specific questions about the Alliance CCV template or submission issues to the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: For specific questions about the Alliance CCV template or issues submitting a CCV to the Alliance, contact support@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to submit a CCV for Alliance purposes?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCV is mandatory for all university Faculty who want to renew their Alliance account and submit an application to any Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: What are the first steps to register for a new CCV account?
qa_pairs.answer: To register, go to https://ccv-cvc.ca/, select your preferred language, click 'Login', then 'Register', and fill out the form.

qa_pairs.question: How do you select the Alliance template when submitting your CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: In the CCV site, go to 'CV' in the top menu, select 'Funding', then choose 'Digital Research Alliance of Canada' as the Funding Source and 'Alliance' as the CV Type, then click 'Load'.

qa_pairs.question: What does a red x-mark next to a field in the Alliance CCV submission list indicate?
qa_pairs.answer: A red x-mark indicates that content is missing or incorrectly filled out for that field, and you need to edit it.

qa_pairs.question: What must you make note of after submitting your Alliance CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: You must make note of the confirmation number assigned upon submission, as it is mandatory for linking your RAC application with your CCV submission on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Can a confirmation number from another CCV template (e.g., NSERC, CIHR) be used for Alliance submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: No, only confirmation numbers for CCVs submitted using the Alliance template will be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: Which sections are mandatory in the Alliance CCV template?
qa_pairs.answer: The mandatory sections in the Alliance CCV template are Personal Information, Education, Employment, and Research Funding History (over the last five years).

qa_pairs.question: How recent must a CCV submission be to renew an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: To renew an Alliance account, your CCV must have been submitted within the last 300 days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial step for Faculty to renew their Alliance account with a CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Faculty must click on https://ccdb.computecanada.ca/account_renewal, scroll to the 'Canadian Common CV' section, and enter their Alliance CCV submission confirmation number.

qa_pairs.question: How recent must a co-PI's CCV be for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: All co-PIs of a RAC application must have an updated CCV on file, meaning it must have been submitted within the last 90 days.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if a co-PI's CCV is not updated within the required timeframe for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The Academic PI will not be able to submit their RAC application until all co-PIs' CCVs meet the 90-day submission requirement.

qa_pairs.question: How can a co-PI update their CCV for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Co-PIs can update their CCV by navigating to the application where they are listed as a co-PI under 'Ongoing Competitions' in the Resource Applications menu, and clicking 'Update CCV', or by going to 'My Account → View Reporting' on CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of reporting publications enabled by Alliance computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Reporting publications helps the Alliance build a stronger case to advocate for additional compute and storage resources and is an input for the scientific review process for RAC applications.

qa_pairs.question: What indicates new publications that require an answer in the 'CCV Publications Reporting' section on CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: New publications for which an answer has not yet been provided are surrounded by a red border.

qa_pairs.question: How do you mark a new publication as 'not enabled by our resources' when reporting?
qa_pairs.answer: You must click twice on the publication: the first click adds a check mark (enabled), and the second click removes it (not enabled).

qa_pairs.question: When is submitting a new CCV optional for an Academic PI with an existing CCV on file for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: If the existing CCV on file was updated within the previous 90 days, submitting a new one for the RAC application is optional.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Academic PI's CCV on file is older than 90 days when applying for RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: If the CCV on file is older than 90 days, the Academic PI will be required to submit a new CCV with their RAC application.
